files.patch:
@@ -32,6 +32,11 @@ beads.left.meta.json
 beads.right.jsonl
 beads.right.meta.json
 
+# Sync state (local-only, per-machine)
+# These files are machine-specific and should not be shared across clones
+.sync.lock
+sync_base.jsonl
+
 # NOTE: Do NOT add negation patterns (e.g., !issues.jsonl) here.
 # They would override fork protection in .git/info/exclude, allowing
 # contributors to accidentally commit upstream issue databases.

@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@ auto-start-daemon: false
 # This setting persists across clones (unlike database config which is gitignored).
 # Can also use BEADS_SYNC_BRANCH env var for local override.
 # If not set, bd sync will require you to run 'bd config set sync.branch <branch>'.
-sync-branch: "main"
+sync-branch: "beads-sync"
 
 # Multi-repo configuration (experimental - bd-307)
 # Allows hydrating from multiple repositories and routing writes to the correct JSONL

@@ -62,6 +62,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-367d","title":"Audit and expand Jargon component terms","description":"# Task: Audit and Expand Jargon Component Terms ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Jargon System & Documentation Polish (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7i45) ## Description The Jargon component provides tooltip definitions for technical terms, but coverage is incomplete and some definitions are too technical. Need a comprehensive audit. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/components/jargon.tsx ### Terms to audit/add: - VPS - Virtual Private Server (already exists, verify quality) - SSH - Secure Shell (verify quality) - Terminal - text interface (verify quality) - Command line - same as terminal (alias?) - Root - superuser/admin account - sudo - run as superuser - Ubuntu user - regular user account - tmux - terminal multiplexer - Session - persistent terminal environment - Repository/Repo - code storage folder - Git - version control system - API key - authentication token - Environment variable - system setting - Port - network endpoint - IP address - network address - zsh - shell program - Bash - shell program ### Quality criteria for definitions: - No jargon in the definition itself - One sentence maximum for tooltip - Relatable analogy if possible - Consistent tone ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All terms listed above have definitions - [ ] Definitions are beginner-friendly (no technical jargon) - [ ] Tooltip text fits comfortably (not too long) - [ ] All wizard pages use Jargon wrappers appropriately - [ ] Mobile tooltip experience is good (tap to show) ## UI/UX Notes - Tooltips should be quick reads (2-3 seconds) - Consider linking to glossary for longer explanations - Test on mobile — touch targets and positioning","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:44:46.425151-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:34:55.414351-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:34:55.414351-05:00","close_reason":"Added 4 new beginner-friendly jargon terms: session, api-key, environment-variable, clone. All terms include short/long descriptions, analogies, and 'why we use it' explanations. Checked for and avoided duplicates with existing terms."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-37rw","title":"EPIC: Learning Hub World-Class UI/UX Overhaul","description":"Transform the Learning Hub section of the ACFS website from its current functional-but-basic state to match the world-class, Stripe-quality UI/UX polish of the main landing page. This includes: **Context & Motivation:** - The main landing page has beautiful animations, gradients, hover states, and cohesive design - The Learning Hub pages look noticeably less polished in comparison ('absolute shit' per user feedback) - Users navigating from the slick landing page to the Learning Hub experience jarring quality drop - This harms user confidence and overall perception of the project **Goals:** 1. Visual consistency with landing page design language 2. World-class desktop experience (sidebars, multi-column, keyboard nav) 3. World-class mobile experience (touch-friendly, gestures, thumb-zone) 4. Framer Motion animations matching landing page quality 5. Progress celebration, gamification elements 6. Polished micro-interactions on every interactive element **Pages in scope:** - /learn (dashboard) - /learn/[slug] (lessons) - /learn/glossary - /learn/commands - /learn/agent-commands - /learn/ntm-palette - /learn/tools/[tool] **Success Criteria:** Users should 'gasp at how stunning and perfect it is in every way'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-25T00:12:33.534668-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T00:55:31.318576-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T00:55:31.318576-05:00","close_reason":"All Learning Hub pages now have world-class UI: motion animations, scroll reveals, floating orbs, stagger animations, hover effects, 48px touch targets, confetti celebrations, keyboard navigation. Pages completed: /learn, /learn/[slug], /learn/glossary, /learn/commands, /learn/agent-commands, /learn/ntm-palette, /learn/tools/[tool]"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-37sa","title":"Add p10k wizard handling instructions","description":"# Task: Add Powerlevel10k Wizard Handling ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np) ## Description On first zsh login, Powerlevel10k may launch a configuration wizard. This is unexpected and confusing for beginners. They don't know what it's asking or if they should configure it. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/reconnect-ubuntu/page.tsx Add a GuideTip or AlertCard: \`\`\`tsx Prompt customization wizard? When you first connect, you might see a "Powerlevel10k configuration wizard" asking about fonts and prompt style. You can:
  • Press q to quit and use defaults (recommended for now)
  • Or go through it if you want to customize your prompt appearance

You can always run p10k configure later to customize.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Warns about possible p10k wizard - [ ] Clear instruction to press 'q' to skip - [ ] Mentions ability to configure later - [ ] Non-scary tone (it's a feature, not a problem) ## UI/UX Notes - Use GuideTip (not warning) — this is informational - Keep it brief; most users will just press q - "Recommended for now" guides beginners to the simpler path","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:43:03.190685-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:35:18.797985-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:35:18.797985-05:00","close_reason":"Added a friendly tip about the p10k wizard (press q to skip; p10k configure later)"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3a81","title":"Add nightly strict installer canary + alert on checksum mismatch","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T10:21:29.878094357-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T10:21:29.878094357-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3bll","title":"Fix Ubuntu upgrade pre-reboot resume stage + target version handling","description":"Bug: pre-upgrade reboot flow stored .ubuntu_upgrade.stage (wrong key) without enabled/target info, so upgrade_resume.sh could not detect the reboot marker and could crash when state lacked upgrade_path. Also upgrade_resume.sh and ubuntu_upgrade.sh hardcoded target 25.10, ignoring --target-ubuntu.\n\nFix: write .ubuntu_upgrade.current_stage=pre_upgrade_reboot + enabled/target_version in install.sh; have upgrade_resume.sh read target_version from state and short-circuit on pre_upgrade_reboot to launch continue_install.sh; make ubuntu_upgrade.sh default target overridable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T01:56:10.854463-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:57:38.519714-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T01:57:38.519714-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed pre-upgrade reboot marker + target version propagation across install.sh, ubuntu_upgrade.sh, and upgrade_resume.sh; added safe short-circuit in resume script."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ci","title":"Fix acfs doctor --json output (pure JSON + correct counts)","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json currently prints section headings before JSON, doesn’t increment pass/warn/fail counters in JSON mode, and doesn’t escape strings. Make --json emit only valid JSON on stdout with correct summary counts (and handle missing /etc/os-release).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:44:19.646755-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json to emit pure valid JSON (no headings), increment counters in JSON mode, and JSON-escape strings; also handles missing /etc/os-release."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3dkg","title":"TASK: Implement info.sh core data gathering","description":"# TASK: Implement info.sh core data gathering ## Context Implement the core data gathering functions for `acfs info`. These read from state files and system commands to populate the info data model. ## Implementation ### File Location `scripts/lib/info.sh` ### Functions to Implement ```bash # Get system hostname info_get_hostname() { cat /etc/hostname 2>/dev/null || hostname } # Get primary IP address info_get_ip() { hostname -I 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}' || echo "unknown" } # Get human-readable uptime info_get_uptime() { uptime -p 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^up //' || echo "unknown" } # Get OS version info info_get_os() { if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then source /etc/os-release echo "${VERSION_ID:-unknown} (${VERSION_CODENAME:-unknown})" else echo "unknown" fi } # Get installation state from state.json info_get_install_state() { local state_file="${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/state.json" if [[ -f "$state_file" ]]; then # Parse with jq if available, fallback to grep ... fi } # Get onboard progress info_get_onboard_progress() { local progress_file="${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/onboard_progress.json" ... } ``` ### Error Handling - All functions should have fallback values - Never fail/exit on missing data - Return "unknown" or empty for missing info ### Performance Requirements - Each function should complete in <100ms - No network calls - No spawning heavy processes ## Testing Create test cases: ```bash # Test with missing files rm -f ~/.acfs/state.json info_get_install_state # Should return defaults # Test with valid files # ... ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All data gathering functions implemented - [ ] Fallback values for missing data - [ ] No network calls - [ ] Each function <100ms - [ ] Works when state files missing","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:56:27.287782-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.632495-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.632495-05:00","close_reason":"Complete implementation: info.sh with terminal, JSON, HTML, minimal output formats; integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3dkg","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-cxz3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:56:44.743105-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]} @@ -439,6 +440,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-n7o","title":"Docs drift: README claims install.sh runs scripts/generated but it doesn't","description":"README architecture diagram/text indicates scripts/generated installer + doctor outputs are wired into install.sh, but install.sh currently contains its own phase logic and doesn't reference scripts/generated. Decide whether to integrate install.sh with generated scripts, or update README to match reality.","notes":"Updated README to clearly state scripts/generated outputs are reference/future and are not invoked by install.sh yet (see commit e891cbe).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T19:16:14.904067-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:09:06.894046-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:09:06.89405-05:00","labels":["architecture","docs"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-natf","title":"services-setup: install Claude guard as target user","description":"install.sh runs services-setup.sh --install-claude-guard during finalize while still root; services-setup currently writes ~/.claude/* directly, resulting in root-owned files in the target user's home. Fix by performing file writes as TARGET_USER (or otherwise ensuring correct ownership) and adding basic symlink hardening.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-31T03:18:17.346629-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:30:34.690042-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:30:34.690042-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed services-setup Claude guard install to write as TARGET_USER, added symlink hardening; validated with bash -n + shellcheck; pushed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-natf","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-wde3","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-31T03:18:17.350578-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","title":"[ubu.6] Integrate upgrade phase into install.sh","description":"# Integrate Upgrade Phase into install.sh ## Purpose Wire the Ubuntu upgrade functionality into the main installer flow as "Phase -1" (before all other phases). ## Changes to install.sh ### New CLI Flags ```bash # In parse_args() --skip-ubuntu-upgrade) SKIP_UBUNTU_UPGRADE=true ;; --target-ubuntu=*) TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION="${1#*=}" ;; ``` ### New Global Variables ```bash # Ubuntu upgrade options SKIP_UBUNTU_UPGRADE=false TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION="25.10" # Default target ``` ### Integration Point Add before existing Phase 0 (preflight): ```bash main() { # ... existing setup ... # Phase -1: Ubuntu Upgrade (before everything else) if [[ "$SKIP_UBUNTU_UPGRADE" != "true" ]]; then run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase fi # Phase 0: Preflight run_preflight_checks # ... rest of phases ... } ``` ### New Function: run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase() ```bash run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase() { # Source upgrade library source "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/ubuntu_upgrade.sh" log_section "Ubuntu Version Check" # Get current version local current_version=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) log_info "Current Ubuntu version: $current_version" # Check if upgrade needed if ubuntu_version_gte "$current_version" "$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION"; then log_success "Ubuntu $current_version meets target ($TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION)" return 0 fi # Calculate upgrade path local upgrade_path=$(ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path "$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION") log_info "Upgrade path: $current_version → $upgrade_path → $TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" # Run preflight checks if ! ubuntu_preflight_checks; then log_error "Preflight checks failed. Cannot proceed with upgrade." log_info "Use --skip-ubuntu-upgrade to bypass (not recommended)" exit 1 fi # Save original args for resume after reboot export ACFS_ORIGINAL_ARGS="$*" # Initialize upgrade state state_upgrade_init "$current_version" "$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" "$upgrade_path" # Setup resume service ubuntu_setup_resume # Start upgrade log_step "Starting Ubuntu upgrade to $TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" log_warn "This will take 30-60 minutes per version and require reboots." log_warn "SSH sessions will disconnect. Reconnect after reboot." ubuntu_do_upgrade # If we get here, reboot is needed ubuntu_trigger_reboot # Script exits here - resume service takes over after reboot } ``` ## Resume Detection When install.sh runs after reboot, detect if we are resuming: ```bash # At start of main() if state_upgrade_get_stage == "awaiting_reboot"; then # This is a resume - upgrade service should handle it # But if user manually runs install.sh, resume gracefully log_info "Detected in-progress upgrade. Checking status..." run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase # Will continue upgrade chain fi ``` ## Documentation Updates Update install.sh header comments with new flags: ```bash # --skip-ubuntu-upgrade Skip automatic Ubuntu version upgrade # --target-ubuntu=VER Set target Ubuntu version (default: 25.10) ``` ## Error Handling If upgrade fails: 1. Log error to /var/log/acfs/upgrade.log 2. Set error state in state.json 3. Print recovery instructions 4. Exit with non-zero code User can then: - Fix the issue manually - Re-run install.sh (will detect failed state and offer options) - Use --skip-ubuntu-upgrade to proceed on current version","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:22:11.33453-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:52.567131-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:52.567131-05:00","close_reason":"Feature fully implemented: CLI flags, run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase(), main() integration, resume detection all in place. Commit 69a4002.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-auf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:23:36.629339-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0ge","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:23:36.876802-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lhe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:43.088899-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-cxr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:43.266262-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-itb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:43.439406-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nb8t","title":"Add release gate that blocks tags if checksums verification fails","description":"Prevent publishing tags/releases when scripts/lib/security.sh --verify has mismatches; also surface clear instructions for updating checksums.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T10:21:34.483958074-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T10:21:34.483958074-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ngtd","title":"Add RU workflow scenario","description":"# Task: Add RU Workflow Scenario to flywheel.ts ## Location File: apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts Array: workflowScenarios (starts ~line 61) Insert after: Last existing scenario ## What Workflow Scenarios Are These are step-by-step narratives showing how tools work together in real workflows. Each scenario has 3-5 steps, each featuring a different tool. ## Exact Code to Add ```typescript { id: 'multi-repo-sync', title: 'Multi-Repo Morning Sync', description: 'Keep 20+ repositories synchronized and commit dirty work automatically with AI assistance.', steps: [ { tool: 'ru', action: 'Sync all repos: \`ru sync -j4 --autostash\`', result: 'All repos cloned, pulled, conflicts detected', }, { tool: 'ru', action: 'Check status: \`ru status --fetch\`', result: 'See ahead/behind/dirty state for all repos', }, { tool: 'ru', action: 'AI commit automation: \`ru agent-sweep --parallel 4\`', result: 'Claude commits dirty repos with intelligent messages', }, { tool: 'bv', action: 'Track remaining work: \`bv --robot-triage\`', result: 'Beads updated across all synced projects', }, ], outcome: '20+ repos synchronized, dirty work committed, ready for agent swarm', timeframe: '15 minutes automated work', }, { id: 'agent-sweep-automation', title: 'AI-Driven Commit Automation', description: 'Let AI agents commit your dirty repos with intelligent, context-aware commit messages.', steps: [ { tool: 'ru', action: 'Preview dirty repos: \`ru agent-sweep --dry-run\`', result: 'See which repos will be processed', }, { tool: 'ntm', action: 'NTM spawns Claude sessions for each repo', result: 'Agents explore and understand each codebase', }, { tool: 'ru', action: 'Execute sweep: \`ru agent-sweep --parallel 4\`', result: 'Agents produce commit plans, RU validates and executes', }, { tool: 'mail', action: 'Agents report completion via Mail', result: 'Full audit trail of what was committed', }, ], outcome: '15 dirty repos committed with high-quality messages in 10 minutes', timeframe: '10 minutes of AI work', }, ``` ## Why These Scenarios 1. **Multi-Repo Morning Sync**: The daily workflow most users need 2. **Agent Sweep Automation**: RU's killer AI feature explained step-by-step ## Verification - Scenarios section on /flywheel page shows new scenarios - Each step correctly references the tool with proper formatting","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T23:00:55.204045189-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:20:38.991058657-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:20:38.991058657-05:00","close_reason":"Added Multi-Repo Morning Sync and Bulk AI Commit Automation workflow scenarios","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ngtd","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0iw7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:32.506703307-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ni52","title":"Add 'Try Your First Command' interactive verification","description":"# Task: Add 'Try Your First Command' Verification ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Terminal Onboarding Foundation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ux1f) ## Description Users need a low-stakes way to verify their terminal works and practice the basic flow: type command, press Enter, see output. This builds confidence before the more complex SSH commands. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/install-terminal/page.tsx Add an interactive section with a simple command to try: \`\`\`tsx
Launch the terminal app you just installed (Ghostty, WezTerm, or Windows Terminal). You should see a blinking cursor after a symbol like $ or %. This is the "prompt" — it means the terminal is waiting for your input. The terminal should respond with: hello If you see "hello" appear, your terminal is working perfectly! You just sent a command to your computer and it responded.
\`\`\` Add a checkbox: "I ran my first command successfully" ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clear step-by-step instructions - [ ] Simple command (echo hello) that always works - [ ] Expected output shown - [ ] Success confirmation/checkbox - [ ] Encouraging tone ("You just sent a command!") ## UI/UX Notes - This should feel like a mini-celebration, not a test - The command must work on all OS (echo works on Mac, Linux, Windows PowerShell) - Consider adding what to do if it doesn't work","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:40:03.439068-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:20:09.312843-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:20:09.312843-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: TerminalBasicsSection component exists and is rendered in install-terminal/page.tsx with echo hello command, expected output, copy/paste instructions, and success state","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ni52","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-grfs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:06.9952-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nic8","title":"Task: Add 'skip for now' reassurance on accounts page","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:49:36.382899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:11:45.832643-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:11:45.832643-05:00","close_reason":"Addressed in parent bead 9dl9 - accounts page now has subscription cost warnings, default-collapsed tiers, and skip-for-now reassurance","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nic8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9dl9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:53:31.398245-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
